key_insight: ZO-Act makes zeroth-order fine-tuning more stable and efficient by perturbing a fixed low-rank activation-derived subspace instead of full weights or random directions.
engineering_relevance: This enables forward-only, memory-light fine-tuning of large and quantized language models while remaining compatible with practical optimizers like Adam.
method: Compute a small activation basis once at initialization for each linear layer.
Optimize only lightweight coefficient matrices in that basis using forward-loss evaluations.
Keep the original weights frozen, which is especially useful for low-bit/INT4 models.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on Llama-3-8B, OPT-13B, and INT4 Llama-3-8B, with consistent improvements over strong zeroth-order baselines on language understanding, QA, and commonsense reasoning tasks.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the chosen low-rank activation subspace matches the true gradient structure, since subspace approximation introduces some bias.
paper_title: ZO-Act: Efficient Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning via One-Shot Activation-Informed Low-Rank Subspaces
